task_completed #236 019d1dd9-625a-7710-8dd6-5d7f5ca66e72 03:20:03
查了，而且能借鉴的项目其实很清楚。 **先给结论** 可以参考，而且你们最值得参考的不是“谁把 GitHub 仓库做成向量库”这一件事本身，而是这 5 个能力怎么组合： - 增量索引 - 结构化切分 - 混合检索 - 图谱关联 - 树形导航 对你们来说，最像目标答案的不是单一项目，而是： `GitNexus + PageIndex + Cognita + selective dense vectors + reranker` **我查到的最值得看的项目** - [Git...
Show task summary

查了，而且能借鉴的项目其实很清楚。

先给结论
可以参考，而且你们最值得参考的不是“谁把 GitHub 仓库做成向量库”这一件事本身，而是这 5 个能力怎么组合：

- 增量索引
- 结构化切分
- 混合检索
- 图谱关联
- 树形导航

对你们来说，最像目标答案的不是单一项目，而是：

GitNexus + PageIndex + Cognita + selective dense vectors + reranker

我查到的最值得看的项目
- [GitNexus](https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus)
它不是简单把仓库文件切块后向量化，而是先做代码结构理解，再做知识图谱，再叠加 BM25 + semantic + RRF 混合检索。README 里明确写了 Tree-sitter 解析、知识图谱、混合搜索、向量索引、MCP 工具链，这个思路对你们很有参考价值。
- [PageIndex](https://github.com/VectifyAI/PageIndex)
这个最像你说的“记忆树 + 探针理论”。它不是向量库路线，而是先生成层级树，再做 tree search。它更适合作为“主线/里程碑/分支”的导航层，不适合作为底层唯一索引。
- [Cognita](https://github.com/truefoundry/cognita)
这个项目最值得借的是“增量索引流程”。它会比较数据源状态和向量库状态，判断新增、更新、删除，再重新解析、切块、嵌入、写回。这个非常适合你们后面用 Gitea/Git 做增量重建。
- [elastic/semantic-code-search-indexer](https://github.com/elastic/semantic-code-search-indexer)
它最有价值的点是“不是所有东西都做 dense embedding”，而是选择性地给更有价值的代码块做 dense vectors。这个思想很适合你们，因为对话也不该全量同权重向量化。
- [codebase-index-cli](https://github.com/dudufcb1/codebase-index-cli)
这个非常贴近你们当前路线：本地索引、SQLite/Qdrant 双存储、Tree-sitter、Git commit 跟踪、实时代码库更新。它说明“Git 变化驱动索引更新”这条路完全可落地。
- [RepoSearchRAG](https://github.com/MarkoKolarski/RepoSearchRAG)
这个项目虽然小，但它把“query expansion + 多策略检索 + listwise reranker + Recall@K 评估”讲得很清楚。对你们后面做“探针拿回来的候选节点怎么排序”很有帮助。
- [claude-context-local](https://github.com/FarhanAliRaza/claude-context-local)
它是比较朴素的一类：本地 embedding + FAISS + SQLite metadata。适合借鉴本地部署和存储组织，但不够回答你们“记忆树”这个更高层问题。
- [Quivr](https://github.com/QuivrHQ/quivr)
它更像通用 RAG 工作流框架，优点是检索流程可配置、支持 reranker、history filter、rewrite。适合借鉴“工作流编排”，不适合直接拿来当记忆树方案。

这些项目说明了什么
GitHub 仓库内容“向量化”目前大致有 4 条路线：

- 纯向量路线
代表是 claude-context-local、部分 codebase-index-cli
做法是文件/代码块切分后直接 embedding。
- 结构化代码理解 + 图谱 + 混合检索
代表是 GitNexus
这是我认为最强、最接近你们未来方向的。
- 增量生产化索引流水线
代表是 Cognita
重点不是怎么搜，而是怎么稳定地持续更新索引。
- 树形索引 / 非向量导航
代表是 PageIndex
这个和你“记忆树”思想最接近。

你们能不能参考？
能，而且我觉得应该这样参考，不要照抄某一个项目。

1. Git/Gitea 负责原始事实和版本线
不是拿来直接当运行时检索树，而是做：
- 原始对话永久留存
- 每轮/每段归档快照
- 分支隔离
- 变更触发增量重建

2. RAG/向量库 负责语义召回
不是全量把所有对话等权做 embedding，而是优先对这些对象做：
- 小归档节点
- 里程碑节点
- 决策节点
- 待办节点
- 实体关系摘要节点

3. 图数据库 负责关系，不负责主线树
图谱很适合管：
- 这个节点属于哪个主题
- 这个里程碑关联哪些任务
- 哪些分支互相有关
- 哪些会话共用同一实体/项目/技术

但“主线收敛结构”最好单独建一棵树，不要全靠图数据库自由游走。

4. 记忆树 本身要做成独立逻辑层
这一层才是你真正的中间件。
它应该至少有：
- main spine：主线里程碑
- branch：某个主题/阶段的分支
- leaf：小归档节点
- source refs：指回原始对话、commit、artifact
- summary + confidence + children + parent

最重要的一点
GitHub 仓库向量化项目和你们的区别在于：

- 代码仓库天然有文件边界、函数边界、AST
- 对话没有天然结构，必须自己定义“压缩单元”

所以你们不能直接照搬“文件 chunk -> embedding”。
你们应该定义的是：

- 3 轮或 1 个闭环任务 = 小归档单元
- 10 个小归档 = 里程碑单元
- 多个里程碑 = 阶段主线单元
- 额外抽取：决策、事实、实体、待办、情绪/目标变化

也就是说，代码库项目给你们的是“索引工程方法”，不是“对话树结构答案”。

我给你的判断
最值得参考的组合是：

- 树形主线：参考 [PageIndex](https://github.com/VectifyAI/PageIndex)
- 图谱关系：参考 [GitNexus](https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus)
- 增量重建：参考 [Cognita](https://github.com/truefoundry/cognita)
- selective embedding：参考 [elastic/semantic-code-search-indexer](https://github.com/elastic/semantic-code-search-indexer)
- Git 驱动索引更新：参考 [codebase-index-cli](https://github.com/dudufcb1/codebase-index-cli)
- rerank/评估：参考 [RepoSearchRAG](https://github.com/MarkoKolarski/RepoSearchRAG)

一句话总结：
你们不该只做“对话向量化”，而该做“Git 事实层 + 记忆树导航层 + 向量召回层 + 图谱关系层”的四层结构。

如果你愿意，我下一步可以直接把这几个项目抽象成一张“OpenMnemo 记忆树架构图”，明确：
- 哪一层借鉴哪个项目
- 你们该新增哪些表/节点类型
- probe_main / probe_branch / probe_leaf 具体怎么实现。

Approval policy is currently never. Do not provide the sandbox_permissions for any reason, commands will be rejected.

<collaboration_mode># Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different <collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode> change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The request_user_input tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.
</collaboration_mode>

<skills_instructions>
## Skills
A skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a SKILL.md file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.
### Available skills
- memorytree-workflow: Detect, initialize, and maintain a reusable MemoryTree project-memory workspace for any code repository. On activation, immediately detect and report the repo's MemoryTree state. (file: C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/SKILL.md)
- openai-docs: Use when the user asks how to build with OpenAI products or APIs and needs up-to-date official documentation with citations, help choosing the latest model for a use case, or explicit GPT-5.4 upgrade and prompt-upgrade guidance; prioritize OpenAI docs MCP tools, use bundled references only as helper context, and restrict any fallback browsing to official OpenAI domains. (file: C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/.system/openai-docs/SKILL.md)
- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)
- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)
### How to use skills
- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.
- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with $SkillName or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.
- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.
- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):
1) After deciding to use a skill, open its SKILL.md. Read only enough to follow the workflow.
2) When SKILL.md references relative paths (e.g., scripts/foo.py), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.
3) If SKILL.md points to extra folders such as references/, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.
4) If scripts/ exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.
5) If assets/ or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.
- Coordination and sequencing:
- If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.
- Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.
- Context hygiene:
- Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.
- Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from SKILL.md unless you're blocked.
- When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.
- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.
</skills_instructions>

# AGENTS.md instructions for d:\demo1\openmnemo <INSTRUCTIONS> # AGENTS.md ## Read Order - Read this file first. - Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`. - Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_to...

# AGENTS.md instructions for d:\demo1\openmnemo

<INSTRUCTIONS>
# AGENTS.md

## Read Order
- Read this file first.
- Read the latest file in Memory/01_goals/.
- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in Memory/02_todos/.
- Read Memory/04_knowledge/ only when needed.
- Read Memory/06_transcripts/clean/ and transcript manifests only when needed.
- Read Memory/03_chat_logs/ and Memory/05_archive/ only when needed.

## Memory Rules
- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.
- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.
- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.
- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.

## Transcript Rules
- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.
- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's Memory/06_transcripts/raw/ mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.
- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.
- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.
- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.
- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep Memory/06_transcripts/raw/** unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.

## Git Rules
- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.
- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (Memory/ and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding Memory/06_transcripts/raw/ until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.
- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer memorytree(<scope>): <subject>, or the repository-required equivalent such as docs(memorytree): <subject>.
- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.
- When auto_push is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to ~/.memorytree/alerts.json.

## Heartbeat Rules
- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.
- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.
- If ~/.memorytree/alerts.json contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.
- If memorytree-daemon is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run memorytree-daemon install.
- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.
- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.

</INSTRUCTIONS>

<environment_context>
<cwd>d:\demo1\openmnemo</cwd>
<shell>powershell</shell>
<current_date>2026-03-24</current_date>
<timezone>Asia/Shanghai</timezone>
</environment_context>

# Context from my IDE setup:

## Active file: Memory/04_knowledge/三份豆包文档统一梳理_2026-03-24.md

## Open tabs:

## My request for Codex:
是这样的，未来每个人都可以通过咱们这个自然语言对话的方式来创作自己的一个产品，就相当于以前的这个程序员他的一个功能，因为有了自然语言，有了大模型，他都非常方便地创造自己属于自己的产品。那么创造产品那就天然的他得需要一个存储的位置，那么Git或者Git up它都是一个很好的保存位置。那么我们，我们如何全量索引它的记忆呢？我们有开源的项目，咱们刚才也提到了是Git N E X U S这一个是索引仓库的，那么我们继续前面的那个交流，就是未来每个人创作产品或者对话交流的时候，他必然。有，不管是做项目也好，还是说真正的做创造产品也好，它都会有一个最终的一个产品，一个最终产品，还有一个是对话交流，也就是我们的一个记忆的一个过程。这是这两个，那么最终完结以后呢，它肯定会产生三个东西。第一个就是咱们这个产品，第二个就是咱们这个对话交流的过程，也就是全量记忆的过程。那么第三个是什么呢？就是这个技能。他这个对话，他肯定要复盘一下，产生一个比较好的，就是一个方法论也好，还是一个技能也好，就相当于现在的Skill一样。这样就是说一个项目或者一组对话，都会有一个有三种类型的东西存在，那么我们索引的时候，那就得运用三种不同的方案去索引。是不是这种情况？如果你认可的话，我们接下来继续讨论这个问题。 看看我们的规划 是不是要重新来规划呢

Output:
---
name: memorytree-workflow
description: Detect, initialize, and maintain a reusable MemoryTree project-memory workspace for any code repository. On activation, immediately detect and report the repo's MemoryTree state.
---

# MemoryTree

Use this skill for repo-level project memory. Do not treat it as an application runtime feature unless the user explicitly asks to productize it.

## On Activation

When this skill is invoked, immediately perform these steps without waiting for further user input:

1. Detect the repo state (`not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`) using `references/project-detection.md`.
2. Check `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`. If pending alerts exist, display them to the user before proceeding.
3. If `not-installed`: run `memorytree init` to scaffold the workspace, then report what was created.
4. If `partial`: run `memorytree upgrade` to add missing pieces, then report what was added.
5. If `installed`: read the active goal, bound todo, and latest chat log. Report the current state (goal summary, todo progress, pending tasks) and ask the user what they want to work on next. If `memorytree daemon status` shows the heartbeat is not registered, ask the user whether they want the recommended current-repository setup via `memorytree daemon quick-start --root <repo>` or only a machine-level scheduler restore via `memorytree daemon install --interval 5m --auto-push true`. Use `memorytree daemon register` only when they explicitly want custom branch, interval, report-port, or worktree settings. Use plain-text questions compatible with all AI assistants, not client-specific UI components.

Do not stop after detection to ask whether you should proceed. Act on the result immediately.

## Core Behavior

1. Detect the repo state before changing files. Use `references/project-detection.md`.
2. If MemoryTree is missing, initialize it with `memorytree init`. If the CLI is not available, create the files from `assets/templates/`.
3. If MemoryTree is partial, upgrade it with `memorytree upgrade` before manual maintenance.
4. If MemoryTree is already installed, maintain only the active goal, bound todo, and current chat log. Use `references/update-rules.md`.
5. Keep the installation compatible with the host repo's branch, PR, CI, and review rules. Use `references/git-policy.md`.
6. Prefer safe defaults: do not add reply suffix requirements unless the host repository already requires them, do not mix code-and-memory commits by default, and do not override branch policy. MemoryTree-only git automation is allowed only inside the isolated flow in `references/git-policy.md`.
7. When locale is not explicit, choose it from repo language first, then system language. Use `references/locale-selection.md`.
8. Keep reply language aligned with user language first, then repo locale. Use `references/response-language.md`.
9. For partial repos, use the non-destructive upgrade path before manual maintenance. Use `references/upgrade-path.md`.
10. When transcript archival is enabled, import transcripts with deterministic code, preserve raw transcripts as evidence in both the current repo mirror and the global archive, generate cleaned Markdown for indexing, keep cross-project backfills and ambiguous ownership cases in the global archive only, and ask once per repository whether raw transcript files may be committed or pushed. Use `references/transcript-archive.md`.
11. When CLI commands are needed, use `memorytree <subcommand>` (requires Node.js 鈮?20). If Node.js is not available, fall back to manual scaffolding from `assets/templates/`. See `references/execution-environment.md`.
12. Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
13. Global configuration is stored in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`. See `references/global-configuration.md`.
14. When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand transcript sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all three clients, and generate a continuation summary. See `references/transcript-archive.md` and `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
15. All interactive prompts (heartbeat install, configuration, session continuity) must use plain-text questions compatible with every AI assistant that supports this skill. Do not rely on client-specific UI components. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.

## Read Order

1. Repo `AGENTS.md`, if present.
2. Latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
3. Latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
4. `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
5. `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/`, transcript manifests, and repo-local raw transcript mirrors only when transcript history or cross-project memory search is relevant.
6. `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Detect

1. Check whether `Memory/` exists and matches the layout in `references/memory-layout.md`.
2. Classify the repo as `not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`.
3. If the repo already has stronger rules than the generated templates, preserve the repo rules and only add missing MemoryTree pieces.

## Upgrade

1. Use `memorytree upgrade` for repositories that already have `AGENTS.md`, custom policy files, or partial `Memory/` content.
2. Preserve existing repo policy files by default.
3. Create only the missing MemoryTree pieces.
4. Read the upgrade result and treat `agents_merge_required=true` as a manual review task, not an overwrite instruction.

## Initialize

1. Run `memorytree init --root <repo>` and pass `--project-name`, `--goal-summary`, and `--locale` when the user already provided them.
2. Use fresh init only when the repo does not already have `AGENTS.md` or another repo policy source such as a policy-bearing `CONTRIBUTING.md`, PR templates, `CODEOWNERS`, or commitlint config. If repo policy already exists, switch to `memorytree upgrade` instead of trying to skip `AGENTS.md`.
3. Review the generated `AGENTS.md` before keeping it. Merge it with the repo's existing policy if needed.
4. Keep the initial installation minimal: `Memory/01_goals`, `02_todos`, `03_chat_logs`, optional `04_knowledge`, `05_archive`, and `06_transcripts` only when transcript archival is enabled for the repo or needed for transcript import.
5. Use the locale-specific template files in `assets/templates/<locale>/` as the source of truth for first-run scaffolding.
6. Prefer `--locale auto` when the user did not request a language explicitly.
7. Use `memorytree locale` when you need a deterministic locale check without scaffolding files.
8. If Node.js is not available, create first-run files directly from `assets/templates/` instead of requiring a runtime installation.
9. If transcript archival is enabled for the repo, use `memorytree import --source <file>` for one explicit source file and `memorytree discover` when scanning local client stores. Matching transcripts must update both the repo mirror and the global archive, while unrelated projects, ambiguous ownership cases, or mismatched explicit sources are archived in the global archive only. Let the CLI generate cleaned transcripts deterministically without spending model tokens on the cleaning step.
10. Ask once whether raw transcript files may also be committed to the repository. Record the answer in `AGENTS.md`, keep the files in the repo either way, and exclude `raw` files from automatic staging until the user says yes.

## Maintain

1. Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a scope or requirement change.
2. Update the active todo when progress changes, milestones move, or the next task becomes clearer.
3. Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
4. Keep active files small. Move stale context to archive or knowledge files instead of bloating the active goal or todo.
5. Treat product ideas, architecture decisions, and operating constraints as goal or knowledge content. Treat step-by-step execution state as todo content.
6. Treat cleaned transcript indexes as discovery aids, not as the final source of truth. When exact wording matters, confirm against the raw transcript archive.
7. Prefer deterministic CLI-based transcript cleaning over manual rewriting. Do not spend model tokens to restate or normalize transcript content when `memorytree import` can do it.
8. Reply in the user's language when clear; otherwise align with repo locale without rewriting existing files just to translate them.

## Git And Safety

1. Never assume MemoryTree can override the repo's branch model.
2. Default to isolated MemoryTree-only Git operations: stage only MemoryTree-owned files, use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title, push to a dedicated branch, and open a dedicated PR.
3. Enable auto-merge only for MemoryTree-only PRs, and only when the repository still enforces its required review and CI checks.
4. Keep repo-local raw transcript mirrors and the global transcript archive synchronized on every import. The global index must remain safe for concurrent multi-project updates.
5. Keep the repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current repository. Unrelated or ambiguous transcripts belong in the global archive only.
6. Keep raw transcript files under `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` in the repo, but exclude them from automatic staging until the user explicitly approved raw transcript uploads for that repository.
7. If the diff mixes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript archives, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before committing or pushing.
8. If the repo has protected branches or PR-only rules, follow them even if older MemoryTree rules say otherwise.
9. When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no Git remote is configured, the push is skipped. If a push fails, the heartbeat retries once. In either case an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`. See `references/git-policy.md`.
10. During transcript cleaning, the heartbeat scans for sensitive information (API keys, passwords, tokens). Matches are logged as warnings only 鈥?no automatic deletion or redaction. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.

11. Do not modify the host repository's CI or E2E workflow definitions by default. If the user explicitly approves a MemoryTree-only optimization, prefer path-filtered or lightweight workflows for `Memory/**` and managed `AGENTS.md` PRs without weakening required branch protection.

## Resources

- `references/project-detection.md`: detect install state and choose init vs maintain.
- `references/memory-layout.md`: folder and file naming rules.
- `references/update-rules.md`: when to version goals, todos, and session logs.
- `references/git-policy.md`: safe Git defaults and policy merge guidance.
- `references/locale-selection.md`: locale choice rules and alias behavior.
- `references/response-language.md`: reply-language precedence and translation safety.
- `references/transcript-archive.md`: raw transcript archival, clean transcript indexing, per-client source rules, and repo upload confirmation rules.
- `references/upgrade-path.md`: safe adoption flow for partial repos.
- `references/execution-environment.md`: how to run MemoryTree CLI (requires Node.js 鈮?20).
- `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`: background heartbeat architecture, daemon CLI, and execution flow.
- `references/global-configuration.md`: `~/.memorytree/` directory layout, `config.toml` schema, and `alerts.json` format.
- `assets/templates/en/` and `assets/templates/zh-cn/`: seed files for English and Simplified Chinese initialization.
- CLI commands: `memorytree init`, `memorytree upgrade`, `memorytree import`, `memorytree discover`, `memorytree locale`, `memorytree recall`, `memorytree daemon install|uninstall|run-once|watch|status|quick-start|register`.

## North Star

浠?OpenMnemo 涓烘暟鎹簳搴э紝鏋勫缓 AI 鍘熺敓鍗忎綔骞冲彴锛圓gentCrawl锛夈€?骞冲彴閲囩敤鍒嗗眰寮€婧愮瓥鐣ワ細鏁版嵁灞?瀛樺偍灞?鎺ㄧ悊灞傚叏閮ㄥ紑婧愬紩娴侊紝鍗忎綔灞備笌鎻掍欢灞傚晢涓氬寲鍙樼幇銆?鐢ㄦ暟鎹富鏉冿紙绉佹湁閮ㄧ讲 Gitea锛夊鎶椾簯绔攣瀹氬瀷绔炲搧锛岀敤鎻掍欢鐢熸€佸舰鎴愮綉缁滄晥搴旀姢鍩庢渤銆?
## 浜у搧鍒嗗眰鏋舵瀯

```

## 璁板繂浣撶郴鎶€鏈灦鏋?
### 涓夎矾娣峰悎妫€绱?
```
鐢ㄦ埛鏌ヨ
    鈫?骞惰涓夎矾妫€绱細
  鈶?SQLite FTS4    鈫?鍏抽敭璇嶇簿纭尮閰嶏紙title/cwd/branch/content/commit_layer锛?  鈶?sqlite-vec     鈫?璇箟鍚戦噺鐩镐技搴︼紙embedding 1536缁达級
  鈶?SQLite 鍥惧叧绯昏〃 鈫?瀹炰綋鍏崇郴杩借釜锛堜汉鐗?椤圭洰/鎶€鏈?姒傚康/session/commit锛?    鈫?RRF锛圧eciprocal Rank Fusion锛夊悎骞舵帓搴?    鈫?杩斿洖 top-N session锛岄檮甯︽憳瑕?+ 鍏宠仈 commit

Phase 0 涓夎矾妫€绱㈠叏閮ㄥ熀浜?`better-sqlite3` 鍗曚竴寮曟搸锛屽叡鐢ㄤ竴涓?.sqlite 鏂囦欢锛岄浂棰濆杩涚▼銆?
### 鍥?Schema

鑺傜偣绫诲瀷锛歚Person` / `Project` / `Technology` / `Concept` / `Session` / `Commit`

鍏崇郴绫诲瀷锛?- `(Person)-[:DISCUSSED]->(Concept)`
- `(Person)-[:WORKED_ON]->(Project)`
- `(Session)-[:CONTAINS]->(Concept)`
- `(Session)-[:RELATED_TO]->(Commit)`
- `(Concept)-[:DEPENDS_ON]->(Technology)`
- `(Concept)-[:EVOLVED_FROM]->(Concept)`
- `(Project)-[:USES]->(Technology)`

### LLM 鎻愬彇灞?
heartbeat 鍚屾鏃讹紝鐢?Haiku 浠庡璇濆叏鏂囨彁鍙栵細
- **鍏抽敭浜嬪疄** 鈫?embedding 鈫?sqlite-vec
- **瀹炰綋 + 鍏崇郴** 鈫?鑺傜偣/杈?鈫?SQLite 鍥惧叧绯昏〃锛坓raph_nodes + graph_edges锛?- **瀵硅瘽鍏ㄦ枃** 鈫?FTS 绱㈠紩 鈫?SQLite FTS

### SQLite 寮曟搸杩佺Щ

Phase 0 鍏抽敭鍙樻洿锛歚sql.js`锛圵ASM锛夆啋 `better-sqlite3`锛堝師鐢熺粦瀹氾級

杩佺Щ鍘熷洜锛?- `sqlite-vec` 鏄?C 鎵╁睍锛岄渶瑕佸師鐢?SQLite锛屾棤娉曞湪 sql.js WASM 涓姞杞?- `better-sqlite3` 鍚屾 API锛屾€ц兘姣?sql.js 蹇?5-10x
- 鏈?prebuild 浜岃繘鍒讹紝CI 鏃犻渶 node-gyp 缂栬瘧
- FTS4 + sqlite-vec + 鍥惧叧绯昏〃鍏辩敤鍚屼竴涓?SQLite 瀹炰緥锛屼簨鍔′竴鑷存€уソ

### 鍥惧瓨鍌ㄦ柟妗堬紙Phase 0锛?
Phase 0 涓嶅紩鍏ヤ笓鐢ㄥ浘鏁版嵁搴擄紙kuzu npm 鍖呭凡搴熷純锛夛紝鏀圭敤 SQLite 鍏崇郴琛ㄦā鎷燂細

```sql
CREATE TABLE graph_nodes (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,       -- 'person:寮犱笁'
  type TEXT NOT NULL,        -- Person | Project | Technology | Concept | Session | Commit
  properties TEXT            -- JSON
);

CREATE TABLE graph_edges (
  source_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  target_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  type TEXT NOT NULL,         -- DISCUSSED | WORKED_ON | CONTAINS | ...
  properties TEXT,
  PRIMARY KEY (source_id, target_id, type)
);
```

Phase 0 鏁版嵁瑙勬ā锛堜釜浜虹敤鎴凤紝<1涓囪妭鐐癸級涓嬶紝SQL JOIN 鏌ヨ涓庝笓鐢ㄥ浘搴撴€ц兘宸窛涓洪浂銆?鍥剧储寮曟槸**娲剧敓鏁版嵁**锛堟簮鑷璇濊褰曪級锛岄殢鏃跺彲鍒犻櫎閲嶅缓锛岃縼绉婚浂椋庨櫓銆?

|------|------------|---------|--------|---------|
| Phase 0锛堜釜浜猴級 | `better-sqlite3` | `sqlite-vec` 鎵╁睍 | SQLite 鍏崇郴琛?| 鍗曟枃浠讹紝闆堕澶栬繘绋?|
| Phase 1锛堝洟闃燂級 | `better-sqlite3` | `Qdrant` | `Neo4j Community` | Docker 鐙珛鏈嶅姟 |
| Phase 1+锛堟墭绠★級 | 鈥?| `Pinecone` / `Weaviate` 绛?| `Memgraph` / `Neo4j Aura` | 浜戞墭绠?|

鎵€鏈夊悗绔疄鐜扮粺涓€鐨?`SearchAdapter` / `VectorAdapter` / `GraphAdapter` 鎺ュ彛锛岄厤缃枃浠朵竴琛屽垏鎹紝涓婂眰浠ｇ爜闆舵劅鐭ャ€俙factory.ts` 鏍规嵁閰嶇疆鍒涘缓閫傞厤鍣ㄥ疄渚嬶紙宸ュ巶妯″紡锛夈€傚崌绾у悗绔椂闇€杩愯杩佺Щ鑴氭湰閲嶆柊 index锛堟暟鎹簮涓嶅彉锛屼粎閲嶅缓绱㈠紩锛夈€?
閫傞厤鍣ㄧ洰褰曠粨鏋勶細

## 鍟嗕笟妯″紡

- **妯″瀷鍙傝€?*锛欸itLab CE/EE 鍒嗗眰妯″紡

## 宸紓鍖栦紭鍔?
1. **鏁版嵁涓绘潈**锛氱敤鎴锋暟鎹瓨鍦ㄨ嚜宸辩殑 Gitea锛屼笉渚濊禆浜戠绗笁鏂?2. **璺ㄥ鎴风褰掓。**锛氬敮涓€鍚屾椂鏀寔 Claude/Codex/Gemini/Doubao 鐨勫紑婧愬伐鍏?3. **Git 鍏宠仈璁板繂**锛氬璇濅笌 commit 鍏宠仈锛岃兘妫€绱€屽仛鏌愪釜鍔熻兘鏃剁殑鎬濊€冭繃绋嬨€?4. **涓夎矾娣峰悎妫€绱?*锛欶TS 鍏抽敭璇?+ 鍚戦噺璇箟 + 鍥惧疄浣撳叧绯伙紝妫€绱㈣川閲忚繙瓒呭崟涓€鏂规
5. **鍙彃鎷斿瓨鍌ㄥ悗绔?*锛歅hase 0 鍏ㄩ儴鍩轰簬 better-sqlite3 鍗曚竴寮曟搸锛團TS + sqlite-vec + 鍥惧叧绯昏〃锛夛紝Phase 1 骞虫粦鍗囩骇鍒?Qdrant/Neo4j锛屼笂灞備唬鐮侀浂鏀瑰姩锛岄噸寤虹储寮曞嵆鍙?6. **AI Chat 鍘熺敓鍏ュ彛**锛氭姤鍛婇〉鍐呭祵 chat widget锛岀敤鑷劧璇█鏌ヨ鑷繁鐨勮蹇嗭紝鍩瑰吇鐢ㄦ埛涔犳儻锛屼负鐙珛 Web 瀹㈡埛绔摵璺?7. **鎻掍欢鐢熸€佸鍨?*锛氬崗浣滃眰 + 鎻掍欢甯傚満涓€鏃﹀舰鎴愶紝杩佺Щ鎴愭湰鏋侀珮

## 绔炲搧瀵规瘮

# Project Todo

## 2026-03-22 Update
- [x] 1.6 graph re-import cleanup landed: managed session subgraph is replaced on transcript re-import, so stale `MemoryUnit / SourceAsset / ArchiveAnchor` nodes no longer linger in runtime graph storage
- [x] graph entity matching was tightened: graph recall no longer matches technical ids, labels, or management metadata such as `memory unit` / `document chunk` by default
- [x] 1.6 graph runtime bridge landed: extraction bundle graph nodes and edges now upsert into the global runtime graph store during `importTranscript`
- [x] graph recall now includes `MemoryUnit`, `ArchiveAnchor`, and `SourceAsset` labels, so unit-level graph nodes can resolve back to sessions
- [x] next focus remains unit-level vector write and recall, then mixed retrieval can move from session-first toward unit-first aggregation
- [x] 1.6 Phase 2 baseline extractor 宸茶惤鍦帮細`importTranscript` 瀵煎叆鏃朵細鍚屾鐢熸垚 `source_asset + memory_units(document_chunk) + archive_anchor + structural graph bundle`
- [x] extracted bundle 褰撳墠鍐欏叆 repo `Memory/06_transcripts/extracted/**` 涓?global `index/extracted/**`
- [ ] 涓嬩竴姝ヤ粛鏄妸 vector / graph 鐨勪富鍐欏叆涓庝富鍙洖瀵硅薄浠?session 绾ч€愭鍗囩骇鍒?unit 绾э紝鑰屼笉鏄仠鐣欏湪 bundle 钀界洏

## 2026-03-23 Update
- [x] 1.6 unit-level vector write/recall landed: `importTranscript` now syncs `memory_units` into an isolated `memory_units` vector namespace, and session re-import replaces the prior managed unit vectors instead of accumulating stale rows
- [x] sqlite vector storage gained namespace separation plus `deleteByMetadata(filter)`, so the new unit vector lane does not pollute legacy `vec_sessions` data and can be managed per session root
- [x] deterministic vector baseline was hardened for multilingual queries: Unicode letters/numbers now survive normalization, and zero-vector queries such as punctuation-only input return no vector hits instead of noisy fallback recall
- [x] mixed retrieval upgrade started: `searchRecall` vector lane now prefers unit-first aggregation, rolling `memory_unit` vector hits back to sessions through extracted bundle metadata while preserving session-level deterministic fallback for older indexes
- [x] higher-level `DataLayerAPI` mixed retrieval now prefers structured `memory_unit / source_asset / archive_anchor` hits and suppresses redundant plain session hits when those structured hits can resolve back to the same session
- [x] graph/session fusion is now consistent with the unit-first direction: graph recall searches `MemoryUnit / ArchiveAnchor / SourceAsset` nodes first, resolves those structured hits back to sessions, and only falls back to generic graph/session entities when structured graph recall is absent
- [x] graph entity matching is further tightened for unit-first recall quality: operational metadata such as `project / client / session_id / cwd / branch / source_ref / source_uri / import_ref` no longer counts as semantic graph search text, which avoids false structured hits and preserves the intended entity-first lane split
- [x] default local runtime wiring for `DataLayerAPI` landed: `createLocalRetrievalTools()` and `createLocalDataLayerAPI()` now bind the local transcript index, unit vectors, extracted bundle catalog, session hydration, commit context lookup, entity graph view, and session pagination into a ready-to-run local entrypoint
- [x] the local runtime now exposes direct `session / memory_unit / source_asset / archive_anchor / mixed` retrieval through one API surface, with structured refs resolving back to sessions by default and list/get helpers backed by the local SQLite index plus extracted bundle artifacts
- [x] 1.6 main-line closure reached for Phase 0: runtime objects, extraction bundles, unit-level vector recall, unit-first graph recall, mixed retrieval hydration, and the default local `DataLayerAPI` entrypoint are all in place; next planning focus can move above the data layer

## 1. 鏍稿績缁撹

杩欎笁浠芥枃妗ｅ悎璧锋潵锛屾寚鍚戠殑骞朵笉鏄€滃仛涓€涓亰澶╄褰曞綊妗ｅ伐鍏封€濓紝鑰屾槸瑕佹妸 OpenMnemo 鎺ㄥ悜涓€涓洿澶х殑瀹氫綅锛?

瀹冪殑浠诲姟涓嶆槸鍙妸瀵硅瘽瀛樹笅鏉ワ紝鑰屾槸鎶婂垎鏁ｅ湪涓嶅悓 AI 骞冲彴銆佷笉鍚屽伐浣滄祦銆佷笉鍚屼汉鐢熼樁娈甸噷鐨勯棶棰樸€佹兂娉曘€佸喅绛栥€佺粡楠屽拰鍋忓ソ锛屾矇娣€鎴愪竴濂楋細

鐨勯暱鏈熻蹇嗙郴缁熴€?
濡傛灉鐢ㄤ竴鍙ユ洿瀹屾暣鐨勮瘽鏉ュ畾涔夛細

> OpenMnemo = 涓€涓紑婧愮殑涓汉 AI 璁板繂搴曞骇锛涚敤 Git 鍖栬蹇嗘爲銆佸師瀛愬寲璁板繂瀵硅薄鍜屾帰閽堝紡妫€绱紝鎶婂垎鏁ｅ湪鍚勫钩鍙扮殑瀵硅瘽銆佹兂娉曘€佸喅绛栧拰缁忛獙锛屾矇娣€鎴愬彲闅旂銆佸彲鍏变韩銆佸彲杩涘寲銆佸彲琚换鎰?AI 璋冪敤鐨勪汉鐢熻蹇嗕腑鏋€?

涓绘枃妗ｆ渶閲嶈鐨勪环鍊硷紝涓嶆槸鏌愪釜鍏蜂綋鍔熻兘鐐癸紝鑰屾槸鎶?OpenMnemo 鐨勪笂灞傜洰鏍囨媺楂樹簡銆傚畠寮鸿皟鐨勬槸锛?

OpenClaw 閭ｄ唤鏂囨。鏈€鏈変环鍊肩殑锛屼笉鏄骇鍝佸澹筹紝鑰屾槸瀹冨鈥滃浣曡皟璁板繂鈥濊繖浠朵簨鐨勭悊瑙ｃ€傚畠寮鸿皟锛?

杩欓噷鏈€鍊煎緱鍚告敹鐨勪竴鐐规槸锛?

Output:
# 2026-03-23 06:00 Session Log

---
Time: 2026-03-23 06:00
User: Asked Codex to continue and perform two review passes.
Assistant: Completed the 1.6 unit-level vector write/recall baseline that had been left in progress. Extended sqlite vector storage with namespace separation and `deleteByMetadata(filter)`, added the isolated `memory_units` vector lane, wired transcript import to sync extracted memory-unit vectors into the runtime index, added memory catalog/vector helpers, and covered the change with adapter, import, and unit-vector regression tests. During review, found and fixed one additional quality issue in the deterministic vector baseline: non-ASCII text such as Chinese could collapse into a zero vector and produce noisy recall, so Unicode-aware normalization and an explicit zero-vector short-circuit were added before finishing.

---
Time: 2026-03-23 06:08
User: Requested that the work continue and be reviewed twice before the next submission step.
Assistant: Performed two review passes. Pass 1 was execution-focused: `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core test`, `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core build`, `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/cli typecheck`, and `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/sync typecheck` all passed. Pass 2 was diff-focused: reviewed the vector namespace isolation, managed cleanup path, import-time vector sync, Unicode/zero-vector handling, and the new regression coverage; no blocking issues were found. Also confirmed that the unrelated user knowledge notes under `Memory/04_knowledge/` and the Doubao transcript export file remained untouched.

---
Time: 2026-03-23 08:46
User: Asked Codex to submit the finished 1.6 unit-vector change to GitHub and then continue into the next stage.
Assistant: Split the finished work into two commits and pushed them to `origin/main`: `1fb44af` (`feat(core): add unit-level memory vector recall`) and `6e641c1` (`docs(memorytree): record 1.6 unit vector landing`). Then immediately started the next 1.6 stage by upgrading `searchRecall` from session-first vector fusion toward unit-first aggregation: the vector lane now resolves `memory_unit` vector hits first, rolls them back to sessions via extracted bundle metadata, and falls back to the older session-level deterministic vector ranking when unit-vector artifacts are absent. Added a targeted recall regression test plus full validation with `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core test -- tests/recall/recall.test.ts`, full `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core test`, `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core build`, and `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/cli typecheck`.

---
Time: 2026-03-23 09:02
User: Asked Codex to review the in-progress follow-up work twice and, if clean, submit it to GitHub while clarifying whether 1.6 is fully complete.
Assistant: Performed two review passes over the new unit-first retrieval work. Pass 1 checked the code changes directly: `searchRecall` now performs unit-first vector aggregation back to sessions, and `createDataLayerAPI().search({ target: 'mixed' })` now hydrates structured hits with session context and removes redundant plain session rows when a richer hit already covers that same session. Pass 2 reran validation with `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core test -- tests/memory/domain.test.ts tests/recall/recall.test.ts`, full `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core test`, `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core build`, and `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/cli typecheck`; no blocking findings were found. Recorded that 1.6 is substantially further along but not yet 100% complete, because graph/session fusion still needs the same unit-first cleanup and `DataLayerAPI` still lacks a default local runtime wiring path.

---
Time: 2026-03-23 10:31
User: Asked Codex to continue along the main line.
Assistant: Finished the remaining graph-side unit-first retrieval work for 1.6. Extended the graph adapter with `findNodesByEntity()`, refactored sqlite graph matching so direct entity-node search is available before session aggregation, and tightened graph search semantics so technical metadata such as `project`, `client`, `session_id`, `cwd`, `branch`, `source_ref`, `source_uri`, and `import_ref` no longer produce false semantic graph matches. Updated `searchRecall` to prefer structured graph nodes (`MemoryUnit`, `ArchiveAnchor`, `SourceAsset`) and roll those hits back to sessions before falling back to generic project/concept/session graph recall. Added regression coverage for direct node lookup and for suppressing plain session graph hits when a richer structured graph hit exists. Two review passes were completed: targeted `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core test -- tests/storage/adapters.test.ts tests/recall/recall.test.ts` plus `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core typecheck`, then full `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core test`, `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core build`, `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/cli typecheck`, and `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/sync typecheck`; all passed. Untracked user files under `Memory/04_knowledge/` and `Memory/doubao_20260321101654_https_github_com_openmnemo_o.txt` remained untouched.

---
Time: 2026-03-23 10:40
User: Asked Codex to review twice again and, if clean, submit to GitHub before the next step.
Assistant: Repeated the two review passes before submission. Pass 1 checked diff boundaries and confirmed that only the intended seven core files plus the active todo/chat log were modified, while the three user-owned untracked files remained untouched. Pass 2 reran validation with `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core test`, `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core build`, `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/cli typecheck`, and `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/sync typecheck`; all passed again. Then submitted the product-code change to GitHub as commit `0c94fbd` (`feat(core): make graph recall unit-first`) on `origin/main`, leaving the MemoryTree update as a separate follow-up commit.

---
Time: 2026-03-23 10:58
User: Asked Codex to continue along the main line after the latest submission.
Assistant: Completed the remaining 1.6 local runtime closure by adding `createLocalRetrievalTools()` and `createLocalDataLayerAPI()` as the default local wiring path for `DataLayerAPI`. The new local runtime binds `searchRecall` session retrieval, unit-vector lookup, extracted-bundle source asset and archive anchor search, session hydration for structured refs, local commit-context lookup, local entity-graph view construction, and cursor-based session listing into one ready-to-run entrypoint. Added a real integration-style regression test that imports transcripts into a temp global root and verifies mixed search, structured direct search, session hydration, entity graph lookup, and session pagination through the local runtime. Two review passes were completed: targeted `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core test -- tests/memory/domain.test.ts tests/memory/local-runtime.test.ts` plus `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core typecheck`, then full `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core test`, `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core build`, `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/cli typecheck`, and `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/sync typecheck`; all passed. This closes the remaining 1.6 Phase 0 data-layer runtime wiring gap, while the unrelated untracked user files remained untouched.

---
Time: 2026-03-23 11:05
User: Asked Codex to review twice again and, if clean, submit the new 1.6 local runtime work to GitHub.
Assistant: Repeated two review passes before submission. Pass 1 reviewed the worktree boundary and the new local runtime implementation itself, including the newly added `packages/core/src/memory/local-runtime.ts`, the export wiring, the integration-style runtime test, and the unchanged user-owned untracked files. Pass 2 reran validation with `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core test`, `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core build`, `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/cli typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/sync typecheck`, and `git diff --check`; no blocking issues were found. Then submitted the product-code change to GitHub as commit `8d4360b` (`feat(core): add local data layer runtime`) on `origin/main`, leaving the MemoryTree update as a separate follow-up commit.

shell_command output=Exit code: 0 Wall time: 0.4 seconds Output: # OpenMnemo [![CI](https://github.com/openmnemo/openmnemo/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/openmnemo/openmnemo...

Output:
# OpenMnemo

[![CI](https://github.com/openmnemo/openmnemo/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/openmnemo/openmnemo/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
[![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@openmnemo/cli)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@openmnemo/cli)
[![Node.js >= 20](https://img.shields.io/badge/node-%3E%3D20-brightgreen)](https://nodejs.org)
[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-blue)](LICENSE)

**Cross-platform AI conversation memory manager.**

Automatically discovers, imports, and version-controls your AI coding sessions 鈥?Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, and Doubao 鈥?then generates a searchable HTML dashboard from your conversation history.

> Named after Mnemosyne, the Greek goddess of memory.

---

## Quick Start

### 1. Install

```bash
npm install -g @openmnemo/cli
```

Verify:

```bash
openmnemo --version
```

### 2. Initialize your project

Run this inside any Git repository you want to track:

```bash
openmnemo init --root . --project-name "my-project"
```

This creates a `Memory/` directory structure in your repo for storing transcripts, notes, and reports.

### 3. Import your AI conversations

Auto-discover and import all local transcripts (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI):

```bash
openmnemo discover --root .
```

To import a specific file manually:

```bash
openmnemo import --source /path/to/transcript.jsonl --root .
```

### 4. Generate an HTML report

```bash
openmnemo report build --root . --output ./Memory/07_reports --no-ai
```

Then open `Memory/07_reports/index.html` in your browser 鈥?you'll see a dashboard with all your sessions, charts, and a knowledge graph.

> Add `--model claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` (and set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`) to generate AI-powered summaries for each session.

---

## Automate with the Daemon

Set up a background heartbeat that automatically syncs new conversations every 30 minutes:

```bash
# Register with the OS scheduler (cron / launchd / Task Scheduler)
openmnemo daemon install

# Check status
openmnemo daemon status

# Run one sync cycle manually
openmnemo daemon run-once

# Remove the scheduled task

assistant #324 final_answer 04:02:43

我认可，而且我认为这会影响规划，但不是“推倒重来”，而是把规划从“技术栈规划”升级成“资产模型规划”。 **判断** 你现在这条线是对的：未来一次完整的自然语言创作，最终沉淀的不是一种东西，而是三种资产。 - `产品资产`：代码、文档、配置、数据、发布物、仓库结构。 - `过程记忆`：对话、决策、分歧、尝试、失败、约束、阶段里程碑。 - `技能资产`：复盘后形成的方法论、操作套路、prompt、workflow、checklist...

Expand full message

我认可，而且我认为这会影响规划，但不是“推倒重来”，而是把规划从“技术栈规划”升级成“资产模型规划”。

判断
你现在这条线是对的：未来一次完整的自然语言创作，最终沉淀的不是一种东西，而是三种资产。

- 产品资产：代码、文档、配置、数据、发布物、仓库结构。
- 过程记忆：对话、决策、分歧、尝试、失败、约束、阶段里程碑。
- 技能资产：复盘后形成的方法论、操作套路、prompt、workflow、checklist、可复用 skill。

但不是三套完全独立的底座，而应该是：

- 一套源事实层：Git/Gitea 作为原始存储和版本真相。
- 三类派生对象层：ProductUnit / MemoryUnit / SkillUnit。
- 一套统一检索层：FTS + vector + graph + tree/probe。

这点很关键。否则你会把“产品、记忆、技能”拆成三套数据库、三套同步逻辑、三套权限模型，后面会非常重。

对 OpenMnemo 现规划的影响
现有主线其实没错，不需要废掉。[goal_v002_20260319.md](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/01_goals/goal_v002_20260319.md)、[todo_v002_001_20260319.md](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/02_todos/todo_v002_001_20260319.md)、以及你现在这份整理稿 [三份豆包文档统一梳理_2026-03-24.md](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/三份豆包文档统一梳理_2026-03-24.md) 是能接上的。代码里也已经有往这边走的基础：[extraction.ts](d:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/core/src/memory/extraction.ts)、[data-layer-api.ts](d:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/core/src/memory/data-layer-api.ts)、[local-runtime.ts](d:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/core/src/memory/local-runtime.ts)。

真正要补的是两个顶层设计：

- 把“产品 / 记忆 / 技能 三类资产”升成正式模型，不再默认只有 session 和记忆。
- 把“记忆树 + 探针检索”升成正式导航层，不再把混合检索看成最终形态。

我建议未来架构明确成这 4 层：

1. Source of Truth
- Git/Gitea 仓库
- 原始对话
- 原始文档
- 原始代码
- 原始 skill 文件

2. Object Layer
- ProductUnit：文件、模块、API、Issue、Commit、Release
- MemoryUnit：问题、决策、约束、尝试、结论、偏好
- SkillUnit：步骤、模式、模板、反模式、检查清单

3. Navigation Layer
- ConversationLeaf -> ArchiveBatch -> MilestoneNode -> StageNode
- probe_main -> probe_branch -> probe_leaf -> hydrate_raw
- 这里的“压缩”不是删内容，而是“压缩进入上下文的路径”

4. Retrieval Layer
- 产品资产：结构检索 + 图关系 + FTS 为主
- 过程记忆：记忆树 + vector + graph + 原文回填
- 技能资产：标签/结构/图关系 + FTS 为主，vector 为辅

这里还有一个必须钉死的原则：

- Git commit tree != runtime memory tree

Git/Gitea 应该是 版本真相层 / 事件源层 / 粗筛入口，不是运行时记忆树本身。运行时记忆树应该是你自己的逻辑树。

外部参考
我看了几个你提到的方向，结论很明确：它们都能参考，但各自只解决一部分问题。

- [GitNexus](https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus)
- 强项是“仓库内容索引成图 + MCP 工具化 + 混合检索”。
- 很适合参考 产品资产 这一层怎么做 repo intelligence。
- 它还有一个很重要的点：很多结构是在索引时预计算，不把探索成本全压给 LLM。

- [PageIndex](https://github.com/VectifyAI/PageIndex)
- 最值得你借鉴的是“层级树索引 + 逐层探查”。
- 这和你说的“记忆树 + 探针理论”非常接近。
- 但它更偏长文档，不是完整的 repo memory system。

- [ZenDB](https://yiminglin18.com/publication/zendb/)
- 我查到的更像研究系统，不是一个成熟 GitHub 产品底座。
- 它的价值在于：从文档集合里提炼语义层级结构，再支持结构化查询。
- 这对“技能资产”的抽取和分析很有启发。

- [Elastic semantic-code-search-indexer](https://github.com/elastic/semantic-code-search-indexer)
- 值得参考的是“定时增量索引、忽略规则、MCP 暴露、面向大仓库工程化”。

- [GitHub Copilot 的语义索引说明，2025-03-12](https://github.blog/changelog/2025-03-12-instant-semantic-code-search-indexing-now-generally-available-for-github-copilot/)
- 说明了仓库语义索引本身已经是主流能力，而且强调“打开仓库就触发索引”。

- [GitLab Semantic Code Search](https://docs.gitlab.com/user/gitlab_duo/semantic_code_search/)
- 很明确：代码转 embedding，放进 vector store，并且对默认分支增量更新。
- 这和你未来的 产品资产 检索层高度相关。

- [GitLab Knowledge Graph](https://docs.gitlab.com/user/project/repository/knowledge_graph/)
- 很适合参考“文件、类、函数、依赖关系”怎么图化。

- [GitHub Stack Graphs 论文](https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.01224)
- 最值得学的是 file-incremental。
- 也就是每次只重建改过的局部子图，不全量重算。

最终建议
所以我的结论是：

- 规划要调整，但不是重写。
- 现在要补的不是更多功能，而是先把“三类资产模型 + 记忆树导航层”写成正式架构。
- 当前 Phase 0/1 的技术实现还能继续走，只是上层目标要改得更清楚。

下一步最合适的动作不是先写代码，而是先把规划补成一份正式设计：
OpenMnemo 三类资产架构：产品 / 记忆 / 技能 + 记忆树 / 探针检索

你如果确认把这个当成正式 scope，我下一步就可以直接帮你整理成一版新的目标草案和待办重排方案。

- 把“产品 / 记忆 / 技能 三类资产”升成正式模型，不再默认只有 session 和记忆。
- 把“记忆树 + 探针检索”升成正式导航层，不再把混合检索看成最终形态。

我建议未来架构明确成这 4 层：

1. Source of Truth
- Git/Gitea 仓库
- 原始对话
- 原始文档
- 原始代码
- 原始 skill 文件

2. Object Layer
- ProductUnit：文件、模块、API、Issue、Commit、Release
- MemoryUnit：问题、决策、约束、尝试、结论、偏好
- SkillUnit：步骤、模式、模板、反模式、检查清单

3. Navigation Layer
- ConversationLeaf -> ArchiveBatch -> MilestoneNode -> StageNode
- probe_main -> probe_branch -> probe_leaf -> hydrate_raw
- 这里的“压缩”不是删内容，而是“压缩进入上下文的路径”

4. Retrieval Layer
- 产品资产：结构检索 + 图关系 + FTS 为主
- 过程记忆：记忆树 + vector + graph + 原文回填
- 技能资产：标签/结构/图关系 + FTS 为主，vector 为辅

这里还有一个必须钉死的原则：

- Git commit tree != runtime memory tree

Git/Gitea 应该是 版本真相层 / 事件源层 / 粗筛入口，不是运行时记忆树本身。运行时记忆树应该是你自己的逻辑树。

外部参考
我看了几个你提到的方向，结论很明确：它们都能参考，但各自只解决一部分问题。

- [GitNexus](https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus)
- 强项是“仓库内容索引成图 + MCP 工具化 + 混合检索”。
- 很适合参考 产品资产 这一层怎么做 repo intelligence。
- 它还有一个很重要的点：很多结构是在索引时预计算，不把探索成本全压给 LLM。

- [PageIndex](https://github.com/VectifyAI/PageIndex)
- 最值得你借鉴的是“层级树索引 + 逐层探查”。
- 这和你说的“记忆树 + 探针理论”非常接近。
- 但它更偏长文档，不是完整的 repo memory system。

- [ZenDB](https://yiminglin18.com/publication/zendb/)
- 我查到的更像研究系统，不是一个成熟 GitHub 产品底座。
- 它的价值在于：从文档集合里提炼语义层级结构，再支持结构化查询。
- 这对“技能资产”的抽取和分析很有启发。

- [Elastic semantic-code-search-indexer](https://github.com/elastic/semantic-code-search-indexer)
- 值得参考的是“定时增量索引、忽略规则、MCP 暴露、面向大仓库工程化”。

- [GitHub Copilot 的语义索引说明，2025-03-12](https://github.blog/changelog/2025-03-12-instant-semantic-code-search-indexing-now-generally-available-for-github-copilot/)
- 说明了仓库语义索引本身已经是主流能力，而且强调“打开仓库就触发索引”。

- [GitLab Semantic Code Search](https://docs.gitlab.com/user/gitlab_duo/semantic_code_search/)
- 很明确：代码转 embedding，放进 vector store，并且对默认分支增量更新。
- 这和你未来的 产品资产 检索层高度相关。

- [GitLab Knowledge Graph](https://docs.gitlab.com/user/project/repository/knowledge_graph/)
- 很适合参考“文件、类、函数、依赖关系”怎么图化。

- [GitHub Stack Graphs 论文](https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.01224)
- 最值得学的是 file-incremental。
- 也就是每次只重建改过的局部子图，不全量重算。

最终建议
所以我的结论是：

- 规划要调整，但不是重写。
- 现在要补的不是更多功能，而是先把“三类资产模型 + 记忆树导航层”写成正式架构。
- 当前 Phase 0/1 的技术实现还能继续走，只是上层目标要改得更清楚。

下一步最合适的动作不是先写代码，而是先把规划补成一份正式设计：
OpenMnemo 三类资产架构：产品 / 记忆 / 技能 + 记忆树 / 探针检索

你如果确认把这个当成正式 scope，我下一步就可以直接帮你整理成一版新的目标草案和待办重排方案。

  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

## My request for Codex:
对，我们要先规划，那按这种形态，我们最终要做成什么呢，就是我们开源部分 ，我们和核，记忆的汇总，检索， 创造的底座。 可以这么说吗

assistant #332 final_answer 04:24:26

可以这么说，但我建议我们把这句话再说得更准一点。

“记忆的汇总，检索，创造的底座”这个方向是对的，但还少了两个关键字：沉淀 和 再调用。
因为我们最终做的，不只是把东西收进来、搜出来，而是把它变成可长期生长的资产。

我建议把开源核心定义成这句话：

OpenMnemo 的开源部分，是一个面向自然语言创作时代的记忆与创造底座。它负责把产品、对话过程、技能三类资产，沉淀为可版本化、可检索、可复用、可演化的长期资产。

如果说得更短一点，可以是：

OpenMnemo 开源核心 = 记忆的沉淀底座 + 检索底座 + 创造底座。

再往产品规划里拆，可以明确成 3 件事：

- 汇总/沉淀
- 把代码、文档、对话、决策、复盘、技能统一收进 Git/Gitea 体系。
- 组织/检索
- 用记忆树、RAG、图关系、全文索引，把这些资产变成能稳定调取的结构。
- 创造/复用
- 让用户继续通过自然语言，在这些资产之上生成产品、迭代产品、沉淀技能。

所以更准确地说，不是单纯“记忆汇总工具”，而是：

一个把“创作结果、创作过程、创作能力”都沉淀下来的开源底座。

如果你认可，我下一步可以直接帮你把这句话展开成一版正式规划框架：
北极星定义 + 开源核心边界 + 三类资产模型 + 后续商业层边界。

assistant #351 final_answer 04:31:11

我看完 `deer-flow` 之后，结论很明确：它对我们有很高参考价值，但参考点不在“记忆怎么存”，而在“创作这件事的运行时到底该长什么样”。 `deer-flow` 2.0 把自己定义成一个 `super agent harness`，核心是把 `sub-agents + memory + sandbox + skills` 组织成一个真正能把事情做完的运行时，而不是只做一个会聊天的 agent。[README_zh](htt...

Expand full message

我看完 deer-flow 之后，结论很明确：它对我们有很高参考价值，但参考点不在“记忆怎么存”，而在“创作这件事的运行时到底该长什么样”。

deer-flow 2.0 把自己定义成一个 super agent harness，核心是把 sub-agents + memory + sandbox + skills 组织成一个真正能把事情做完的运行时，而不是只做一个会聊天的 agent。[README_zh](https://github.com/bytedance/deer-flow/blob/main/README_zh.md) 里最值得我们借的几件事是：
- 它把“创作”理解成完整执行链，而不是问答。仓库明确强调从 Deep Research 走向 Super Agent Harness。[README_zh](https://github.com/bytedance/deer-flow/blob/main/README_zh.md)
- 它把 workspace / uploads / outputs 做成线程隔离的工作空间，说明创作底座必须有自己的“工作台”和“产物区”，不能只有上下文窗口。[README_zh](https://github.com/bytedance/deer-flow/blob/main/README_zh.md)
- 它把 skills 当成一级对象，而且按需加载，这点和你说的“技能资产”非常契合。[README_zh](https://github.com/bytedance/deer-flow/blob/main/README_zh.md)
- 它有统一网关，把 models / MCP / skills / memory / uploads / artifacts 放在同一运行平面里，这很像我们未来要的创造入口。[backend architecture](https://github.com/bytedance/deer-flow/tree/main/backend)

但它也有边界。deer-flow 更强的是“执行 harness”，不是“Git-native 长期记忆底座”。它的 memory 更偏用户偏好、事实、工作习惯；而我们要做的是把 创作结果 / 创作过程 / 创作能力 变成长期资产，并且和 Git/Gitea、记忆树、RAG、图关系绑在一起。所以它不能替代 OpenMnemo，但能帮助我们重新定义“创造底座”。

我现在更倾向于把“创造底座”重新定义成这句话：

创造底座，不是让 AI 帮用户答题，而是让用户通过自然语言，持续地产生产物、沉淀过程、提炼能力，并把这三类资产都收回到自己的仓库里。

所以 OpenMnemo 的开源核心，最终不该只叫“记忆底座”，而应该是：

一个 Git-native 的记忆与创造内核。

它至少要有 5 个核心部分：
- 工作空间底座：每个项目/任务都有自己的 workspace、输入区、输出区。
- 执行底座：模型、工具、sandbox、sub-agents、工作流编排。
- 记忆底座：把创作过程压成记忆树，用 probe 逐层调取。
- 资产底座：把产物正式分成 产品资产 / 过程记忆 / 技能资产。
- 回写底座：最终把结果、过程、技能都版本化回到 Git/Gitea。

这样一来，我们和 deer-flow 的关系就很清楚了：

- deer-flow 回答的是：agent 如何真正做事
- OpenMnemo 要回答的是：做完的事情如何沉淀成长期资产
- 我们要补出来的“创造底座”回答的是：如何把做事和沉淀打通成一个闭环

所以我建议我们下一步正式把开源核心改写成一句更完整的话：

OpenMnemo 开源核心 = 面向自然语言创作时代的 Git-native 记忆与创造内核，负责沉淀创作结果、组织创作过程、提炼创作能力。

如果沿这个定义继续，下一步我可以直接帮你把“创造底座”的正式分层也写出来：
运行时层 / 工作空间层 / 资产层 / 记忆层 / 回写层。

deer-flow 2.0 把自己定义成一个 super agent harness，核心是把 sub-agents + memory + sandbox + skills 组织成一个真正能把事情做完的运行时，而不是只做一个会聊天的 agent。[README_zh](https://github.com/bytedance/deer-flow/blob/main/README_zh.md) 里最值得我们借的几件事是：
- 它把“创作”理解成完整执行链，而不是问答。仓库明确强调从 Deep Research 走向 Super Agent Harness。[README_zh](https://github.com/bytedance/deer-flow/blob/main/README_zh.md)
- 它把 workspace / uploads / outputs 做成线程隔离的工作空间，说明创作底座必须有自己的“工作台”和“产物区”，不能只有上下文窗口。[README_zh](https://github.com/bytedance/deer-flow/blob/main/README_zh.md)

创造底座，不是让 AI 帮用户答题，而是让用户通过自然语言，持续地产生产物、沉淀过程、提炼能力，并把这三类资产都收回到自己的仓库里。

- 工作空间底座：每个项目/任务都有自己的 workspace、输入区、输出区。

- 资产底座：把产物正式分成 产品资产 / 过程记忆 / 技能资产。
- 回写底座：最终把结果、过程、技能都版本化回到 Git/Gitea。

Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

assistant #366 final_answer 05:05:03

短答： **现在还不具备 deer-flow 的完整能力。** **但如果我们走对定位，长期可以比 deer-flow 更有价值。** 关键在于，不能用“我们也做一个 super agent harness”来理解自己。 截至 **2026 年 3 月 24 日**，`deer-flow` 明确把自己定位成 `super agent harness`，重点是 `sub-agents + sandbox + skills + mem...

Expand full message

短答：

现在还不具备 deer-flow 的完整能力。
但如果我们走对定位，长期可以比 deer-flow 更有价值。

关键在于，不能用“我们也做一个 super agent harness”来理解自己。

截至 2026 年 3 月 24 日，deer-flow 明确把自己定位成 super agent harness，重点是 sub-agents + sandbox + skills + memory + message gateway，目标是让 agent 真正去“研究、编码、创作”，并处理从几分钟到几小时的任务。[deer-flow README](https://github.com/bytedance/deer-flow) [README_zh](https://github.com/bytedance/deer-flow/blob/main/README_zh.md)

而我们当前的 OpenMnemo，真实已经具备的是：
- 记忆抽取与对象化：[extraction.ts](d:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/core/src/memory/extraction.ts)
- 统一数据层接口：[data-layer-api.ts](d:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/core/src/memory/data-layer-api.ts)
- 本地运行时与混合检索入口：[local-runtime.ts](d:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/core/src/memory/local-runtime.ts)
- 当前主线仍然是“数据层/记忆层做厚”，不是 deer-flow 那种执行 harness。[goal_v002_20260319.md](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/01_goals/goal_v002_20260319.md) [todo_v002_001_20260319.md](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/02_todos/todo_v002_001_20260319.md)

所以要很诚实地说：

- 功能层面
- deer-flow 现在明显更强，尤其是任务执行、沙箱、多 agent 编排、技能运行时。
- 战略层面
- OpenMnemo 有机会更大，因为我们可以做的是“长期资产底座”，不是一次性任务执行器。

我更认可这样的判断：

deer-flow 强在“把事做完”。
OpenMnemo 如果做成，强在“做完之后，这个人的一切创作资产都沉淀下来，并且越用越值钱”。

这就是为什么我们有可能比它更有前景。因为长期价值不只来自执行力，还来自资产复利：

- 创作结果 会沉淀成产品资产
- 创作过程 会沉淀成记忆树
- 创作能力 会沉淀成技能与方法论
- 而且全部回写到 Git/Gitea，归用户自己拥有

如果做到这一步，OpenMnemo 的位置就不是 deer-flow 的同类，而是更像：

所有 agent、所有创作流程、所有项目最终都要回写的 Git-native 资产内核。

所以我的结论是：

- 现在：我们还不具备 deer-flow 的完整功能，不能这么说。
- 未来：我们有机会比 deer-flow 更有价值，但前提不是去正面复制它，而是把自己做成：
- 执行层之上的资产层
- 聊天工具之上的长期记忆层
- 单次任务之上的创作操作系统

最关键的一句话可以定成：

deer-flow 是创作执行引擎，OpenMnemo 应该成为创作资产内核。

如果你认可这个判断，下一步我们就可以正式定义：
OpenMnemo 开源核心，到底包含 deer-flow 式执行层的哪些部分，哪些不做，哪些以后兼容。

task_completed #368 019d1e3a-2d2c-7fc2-817c-f634c27ed2c8 05:05:06

现在还不具备 deer-flow 的完整能力。
但如果我们走对定位，长期可以比 deer-flow 更有价值。

关键在于，不能用“我们也做一个 super agent harness”来理解自己。

截至 2026 年 3 月 24 日，deer-flow 明确把自己定位成 super agent harness，重点是 sub-agents + sandbox + skills + memory + message gateway，目标是让 agent 真正去“研究、编码、创作”，并处理从几分钟到几小时的任务。[deer-flow README](https://github.com/bytedance/deer-flow) [README_zh](https://github.com/bytedance/deer-flow/blob/main/README_zh.md)

而我们当前的 OpenMnemo，真实已经具备的是：
- 记忆抽取与对象化：[extraction.ts](d:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/core/src/memory/extraction.ts)
- 统一数据层接口：[data-layer-api.ts](d:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/core/src/memory/data-layer-api.ts)
- 本地运行时与混合检索入口：[local-runtime.ts](d:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/core/src/memory/local-runtime.ts)
- 当前主线仍然是“数据层/记忆层做厚”，不是 deer-flow 那种执行 harness。[goal_v002_20260319.md](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/01_goals/goal_v002_20260319.md) [todo_v002_001_20260319.md](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/02_todos/todo_v002_001_20260319.md)

所以要很诚实地说：

- 功能层面
- deer-flow 现在明显更强，尤其是任务执行、沙箱、多 agent 编排、技能运行时。
- 战略层面
- OpenMnemo 有机会更大，因为我们可以做的是“长期资产底座”，不是一次性任务执行器。

我更认可这样的判断：

deer-flow 强在“把事做完”。
OpenMnemo 如果做成，强在“做完之后，这个人的一切创作资产都沉淀下来，并且越用越值钱”。

这就是为什么我们有可能比它更有前景。因为长期价值不只来自执行力，还来自资产复利：

- 创作结果 会沉淀成产品资产
- 创作过程 会沉淀成记忆树
- 创作能力 会沉淀成技能与方法论
- 而且全部回写到 Git/Gitea，归用户自己拥有

如果做到这一步，OpenMnemo 的位置就不是 deer-flow 的同类，而是更像：

所有 agent、所有创作流程、所有项目最终都要回写的 Git-native 资产内核。

所以我的结论是：

- 现在：我们还不具备 deer-flow 的完整功能，不能这么说。
- 未来：我们有机会比 deer-flow 更有价值，但前提不是去正面复制它，而是把自己做成：
- 执行层之上的资产层
- 聊天工具之上的长期记忆层
- 单次任务之上的创作操作系统

最关键的一句话可以定成：

deer-flow 是创作执行引擎，OpenMnemo 应该成为创作资产内核。

如果你认可这个判断，下一步我们就可以正式定义：
OpenMnemo 开源核心，到底包含 deer-flow 式执行层的哪些部分，哪些不做，哪些以后兼容。

task_started #369 019d1e3e-d85a-7ed0-9424-1a4a47db059d 05:08:48
task_started

Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

# Context from my IDE setup:

## Active file: Memory/04_knowledge/三份豆包文档统一梳理_2026-03-24.md

## Open tabs:
- 三份豆包文档统一梳理_2026-03-24.md: Memory/04_knowledge/三份豆包文档统一梳理_2026-03-24.md

## My request for Codex:
认可，对我们openmnemo应该成为创造资产的内核。在这个基础上，我们再去衍生我们的商业产品。包括不限于deer-flow 可以这么理解吗

token_count #372 05:08:53
total_tokens=3525868
total 3525868in 4493102out 95418reason 29628
assistant #373 final_answer 05:09:04

可以，这个理解我认可，而且我觉得这是我们现在最该钉死的定位。

更准确地说：

OpenMnemo 不是先定义成某一个具体产品。
OpenMnemo 应该先定义成“创造资产的内核”。
然后在这个内核之上，再长出不同形态的商业产品。

这意味着：

- 开源核心 解决的是资产沉淀与调用
- 创作结果
- 创作过程
- 创作能力
- 商业产品 解决的是不同场景下的使用方式
- 类 deer-flow 的执行型产品
- 面向个人的 AI 创作工作台
- 面向团队的协作产品
- 面向企业的知识与流程产品
- skill 市场、agent 市场、创作市场

所以 deer-flow 在这里更适合被看成：

一种上层产品形态的参考，甚至未来可以是我们衍生出的一个产品方向。

而不是 OpenMnemo 本体。

我会把这个关系说成一句很清楚的话：

OpenMnemo 是底层内核，deer-flow 类产品是上层应用。

或者再完整一点：

OpenMnemo 负责把创造资产收进来、组织好、检索出、再调用；上层商业产品负责把这些资产变成具体可感知的工作流、界面和服务。

所以你现在这句话可以正式成立：

OpenMnemo 应该成为创造资产的内核，在这个基础上再衍生商业产品，deer-flow 类形态只是其中一种。

下一步如果继续往下聊，最关键的问题就变成了：

这个“创造资产内核”的开源边界到底画在哪里。

也就是哪些必须放进开源核心，哪些应该留给上层产品。
这个边界一旦画清楚，后面的规划就会非常顺。

total_tokens=3604437
total 3604437in 4571241out 96278reason 29628

assistant #381 commentary 05:11:08

我继续往最关键的一层收：把“创造资产内核”的开源边界画出来。这个边界一旦清楚，我们后面无论做 deer-flow 类产品、团队协作产品，还是 skill 市场，都会很顺。

assistant #382 final_answer 05:11:27

那我们现在就把最关键的问题钉住： **OpenMnemo 开源核心，到底是什么，边界在哪里。** 我建议直接定成这句话： **OpenMnemo 开源核心 = 创造资产内核。** **它负责把创作结果、创作过程、创作能力，沉淀成用户自己可拥有、可检索、可复用、可演化的长期资产。** 然后我们把边界拆成两部分。 **一、开源核心必须包含什么** 这些东西如果不放进开源核心，OpenMnemo 就不再是“内核”，而只是某个应用。 - ...

Expand full message

那我们现在就把最关键的问题钉住：

OpenMnemo 开源核心，到底是什么，边界在哪里。

我建议直接定成这句话：

OpenMnemo 开源核心 = 创造资产内核。
它负责把创作结果、创作过程、创作能力，沉淀成用户自己可拥有、可检索、可复用、可演化的长期资产。

然后我们把边界拆成两部分。

一、开源核心必须包含什么
这些东西如果不放进开源核心，OpenMnemo 就不再是“内核”，而只是某个应用。

- 统一资产模型
- ProductAsset
- MemoryAsset
- SkillAsset
- 统一导入与沉淀
- 对话、代码、文档、工作流结果都能进入同一套资产体系
- Git/Gitea 原生版本化
- 所有资产最终都能回写到用户自己的仓库
- 记忆树与探针检索
- 不是只做 summary，而是做树形组织、渐进展开、原文回填
- 统一检索层
- FTS
- vector
- graph
- tree/probe
- 技能对象化
- skill 不只是文件，而是可索引、可复用、可关联来源过程的资产
- 最小运行时接口
- 让上层产品能调用这个内核
- 但不是把完整 deer-flow 都塞进内核

所以开源核心本质上要解决的是：

怎么把创造这件事，变成资产。

二、开源核心不应该急着包含什么
这些更像上层产品，或者商业层。

- 完整 deer-flow 式 super agent harness
- 复杂多 agent 编排 UI
- 企业协作权限系统
- SaaS 托管能力
- 商业化 skill 市场
- 团队审计、计费、组织治理
- 重交互工作台
- 无限画布
- 专业项目 cockpit
- 商业工作流编排台

这些不是不做，而是：

它们应该长在 OpenMnemo 内核之上，而不是先把内核做成它们。

三、所以 OpenMnemo 的真正分层应该是
我建议我们以后统一按这个分层思考：

1. 内核层
- 创造资产模型
- Git/Gitea 存储
- 记忆树
- 检索
- 技能沉淀
- 回写

2. 运行时层
- 模型接入
- 工具接入
- 最小 workflow 执行
- 资产生成接口

3. 产品层
- deer-flow 类执行产品
- AI 创作工作台
- 团队协作产品
- 企业知识产品

4. 商业层
- 托管服务
- 权限
- 市场
- 计费
- 组织治理

四、这意味着 deer-flow 在我们体系里的位置
现在可以正式定义成：

- deer-flow 不是 OpenMnemo 本体
- deer-flow 更像我们未来可以衍生的一种上层产品形态
- OpenMnemo 给这类产品提供资产底座

也就是说：

deer-flow 解决“怎么做事”。
OpenMnemo 解决“做过的事怎么变成长期资产”。

如果以后我们做自己的 deer-flow 类产品，它本质上也应该建立在 OpenMnemo 内核之上。

五、下一步我们该聊什么
我建议下一步不要再泛泛讨论了，直接进入正式规划的第一张图：

“创造资产内核”的对象模型。

也就是先定义这三类资产最小长什么样：

- ProductAsset 里最少有哪些字段和关系
- MemoryAsset 怎么从对话压成记忆树
- SkillAsset 怎么从过程里提炼出来

这个一旦定义清楚，后面的技术路线、开源边界、商业边界都会自然出来。

如果你愿意，我们下一轮就直接开始画这三个资产对象。

OpenMnemo 开源核心，到底是什么，边界在哪里。

我建议直接定成这句话：

OpenMnemo 开源核心 = 创造资产内核。
它负责把创作结果、创作过程、创作能力，沉淀成用户自己可拥有、可检索、可复用、可演化的长期资产。

然后我们把边界拆成两部分。

一、开源核心必须包含什么
这些东西如果不放进开源核心，OpenMnemo 就不再是“内核”，而只是某个应用。

- 统一资产模型
- ProductAsset
- MemoryAsset
- SkillAsset
- 统一导入与沉淀
- 对话、代码、文档、工作流结果都能进入同一套资产体系
- Git/Gitea 原生版本化
- 所有资产最终都能回写到用户自己的仓库
- 记忆树与探针检索
- 不是只做 summary，而是做树形组织、渐进展开、原文回填
- 统一检索层
- FTS
- vector
- graph
- tree/probe
- 技能对象化
- skill 不只是文件，而是可索引、可复用、可关联来源过程的资产
- 最小运行时接口
- 让上层产品能调用这个内核
- 但不是把完整 deer-flow 都塞进内核

所以开源核心本质上要解决的是：

怎么把创造这件事，变成资产。

二、开源核心不应该急着包含什么
这些更像上层产品，或者商业层。

- 完整 deer-flow 式 super agent harness
- 复杂多 agent 编排 UI
- 企业协作权限系统
- SaaS 托管能力
- 商业化 skill 市场
- 团队审计、计费、组织治理
- 重交互工作台
- 无限画布
- 专业项目 cockpit
- 商业工作流编排台

这些不是不做，而是：

它们应该长在 OpenMnemo 内核之上，而不是先把内核做成它们。

三、所以 OpenMnemo 的真正分层应该是
我建议我们以后统一按这个分层思考：

1. 内核层
- 创造资产模型
- Git/Gitea 存储
- 记忆树
- 检索
- 技能沉淀
- 回写

2. 运行时层
- 模型接入
- 工具接入
- 最小 workflow 执行
- 资产生成接口

3. 产品层
- deer-flow 类执行产品
- AI 创作工作台
- 团队协作产品
- 企业知识产品

4. 商业层
- 托管服务
- 权限
- 市场
- 计费
- 组织治理

四、这意味着 deer-flow 在我们体系里的位置
现在可以正式定义成：

- deer-flow 不是 OpenMnemo 本体
- deer-flow 更像我们未来可以衍生的一种上层产品形态
- OpenMnemo 给这类产品提供资产底座

也就是说：

deer-flow 解决“怎么做事”。
OpenMnemo 解决“做过的事怎么变成长期资产”。

如果以后我们做自己的 deer-flow 类产品，它本质上也应该建立在 OpenMnemo 内核之上。

五、下一步我们该聊什么
我建议下一步不要再泛泛讨论了，直接进入正式规划的第一张图：

“创造资产内核”的对象模型。

也就是先定义这三类资产最小长什么样：

- ProductAsset 里最少有哪些字段和关系
- MemoryAsset 怎么从对话压成记忆树
- SkillAsset 怎么从过程里提炼出来

这个一旦定义清楚，后面的技术路线、开源边界、商业边界都会自然出来。

如果你愿意，我们下一轮就直接开始画这三个资产对象。

Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",

    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

# 三份豆包文档统一梳理
> 版本：v0.1
> 日期：2026-03-24
> 主文档：`Memory/doubao_20260321101654_https_github_com_openmnemo_o.txt`
> 辅助文档：`Memory/doubao_20260316154210_为什么说_Chat_Nio_有这个通讯的短板呢_为什么说它比.txt`
> 辅助文档：`Memory/doubao_20260316153420_是这样的_OpenClaw就是O_P_E_N_C_L_A_W.txt`

---

## 1. 核心结论

这三份文档合起来，指向的并不是“做一个聊天记录归档工具”，而是要把 OpenMnemo 推向一个更大的定位：

**OpenMnemo 应该成为一个开源的个人 AI 记忆底座 / 记忆中枢。**

它的任务不是只把对话存下来，而是把分散在不同 AI 平台、不同工作流、不同人生阶段里的问题、想法、决策、经验和偏好，沉淀成一套：

- 可归档
- 可检索
- 可隔离
- 可共享
- 可进化
- 可被任意 AI 调用

的长期记忆系统。

如果用一句更完整的话来定义：

> OpenMnemo = 一个开源的个人 AI 记忆底座；用 Git 化记忆树、原子化记忆对象和探针式检索，把分散在各平台的对话、想法、决策和经验，沉淀成可隔离、可共享、可进化、可被任意 AI 调用的人生记忆中枢。

---

## 2. 三份文档分别提供了什么

## 2.1 主文档提供的是北极星

主文档最重要的价值，不是某个具体功能点，而是把 OpenMnemo 的上层目标拉高了。它强调的是：

- OpenMnemo 不应停留在“聊天记录仓库”。
- 它应该成为跨平台、跨客户端、跨场景的统一记忆底座。
- 记忆不只是“存储”，而应该是“可被调用的运行时能力”。
- 底座必须保持开放、通用、场景无关，不能过早绑死在某一种 UI、某一种 Agent、某一种商业形态上。
- 在这个底座之上，可以继续长出统一聊天入口、插件体系、IM 接入、Agent 协同、主动提醒、周期复盘等上层能力。
- 长期愿景不是“我帮你记住”，而是“我持续帮你成为更理想的自己”。

主文档因此提供了几个关键关键词：

- 记忆中枢
- 统一入口
- 心跳机制
- 隔离与共享
- 数字分身
- 理想自我

这些词共同说明，OpenMnemo 的目标是“人生记忆基础设施”，不是“聊天导出工具”。

## 2.2 OpenClaw 文档提供的是记忆检索方法论

OpenClaw 那份文档最有价值的，不是产品外壳，而是它对“如何调记忆”这件事的理解。它强调：

- 记忆应该分层组织，而不是一锅端。
- 检索应该渐进展开，而不是一上来全量召回。
- 最理想的状态是“命中即停”，也就是 hit-and-stop。
- 调记忆更像探针、钻探、针灸，而不是把整个大脑都搬出来。
- 检索需要一套可控的判断机制，而不是完全交给模型自由发挥。

这套思路对于 OpenMnemo 很重要，因为它决定了未来的记忆系统应该是：

- 可控召回
- 分层展开
- 小步逼近
- 命中即止
- 优先精确，不追求一口气灌满上下文

换句话说，OpenClaw 提供的是“记忆引擎的工作方式”。

## 2.3 Chat Nio 文档提供的是应用层和交互层启发

Chat Nio 那份文档真正有价值的，是它对“用户如何和复杂记忆系统交互”的想法。它提供了几个重要启发：

- 无限画布和节点化交互，适合承接复杂问题的拆解、版本演化和多轮推演。
- `X6 + Fabric` 这样的图形交互壳，适合做可视化工作台。
- `React AI SDK` 更适合作为前端 AI 交互层的补充，而不是替代 OpenMnemo 这种底座。
- 用户的原始问题本身就是高价值资产，甚至比 AI 回答更值得长期保存。
- 应该能沿着问题历史，追踪需求变化、关键转折点、不同版本方案和总结视角。

这里最值得吸收的一点是：

> 在长期记忆产品里，用户提出过什么问题、为什么会问这个问题、这个问题后来如何演化，本身就是核心资产。

所以 Chat Nio 提供的是“应用层产品化表达方式”，不是底座本身。

---

## 3. 三份文档合并后的完整图景

如果把三份文档拼起来，可以得到一个更完整的 OpenMnemo 分层图景。

## 3.1 底座层：开源、通用、可复用的记忆底座

这一层是 OpenMnemo 最核心的定位，应该解决的是：

- 跨平台对话、文档、资料、想法的统一沉淀
- Git 化归档和可追溯历史
- 统一的记忆对象模型
- 统一的检索和调用接口
- 隔离、共享、脱敏、权限边界

这一层不能被某个具体 UI 或某个具体商业产品绑住，否则底座会过早失去扩展性。

## 3.2 记忆对象层：从 session 走向更细粒度的 memory unit

三份文档共同暗示了一件事：

真正可复用的，不只是整段 session，而是更小粒度的记忆对象，例如：

- 事实
- 决策
- 约束
- 偏好
- 任务
- 洞见
- 文档片段
- 归档锚点

也就是说，OpenMnemo 的长期方向不是“只存 session”，而是把 session、文档和其他来源进一步提炼成可独立召回、可关联、可演化的记忆单元。

## 3.3 检索层：分层检索 + 探针式检索 + 混合检索

从 OpenClaw 的启发看，OpenMnemo 的检索最好不是一次性全量灌入，而是：

- 先粗召回
- 再局部探测
- 再根据命中结果逐层展开
- 命中即止

配合 OpenMnemo 当前已经在推进的 FTS / vector / graph 混合检索，可以形成更稳定的方向：

- FTS 负责精确文本入口
- vector 负责语义接近
- graph 负责实体与关系联想
- probe-style retrieval 负责控制展开深度和召回节奏

这样，记忆系统就不只是“搜得到”，而是“搜得准、搜得稳、搜得可控”。

## 3.4 交互层：统一聊天入口，但不止于聊天

主文档和 Chat Nio 文档都在指向同一个方向：

OpenMnemo 需要一个统一入口，让用户能够用自然语言直接调用自己的全部记忆。

但这个入口未来不一定只有一种形态，它可以同时存在于：

- 报告页里的 AI Chat
- 独立 Web 客户端
- IDE/插件
- IM 机器人
- Agent 工作流
- 无限画布式工作台

统一入口的意义，不是做一个聊天框，而是让所有上层交互最终都回到同一套记忆底座之上。

## 3.5 治理与进化层：隔离、共享、心跳、周期复盘

主文档里很关键的一点，是它不是只讨论“记忆怎么存”，而是在讨论“记忆怎么长期演化”。这里至少有四个方向：

- 不同人生分区、项目分区、身份分区之间的隔离
- 在受控前提下的共享和授权
- heartbeat 驱动的周期清洗、提炼、压缩、复盘
- 让 AI 不只是被动回答，而是逐步形成对用户长期目标的持续协助

这说明 OpenMnemo 的长期能力不止是 recall，还包括 maintenance 和 evolution。

## 3.6 产品层：从记忆工具走向“理想自我”的支持系统

三份文档最终收束到的，不是一个单纯效率工具，而是一个更高层的产品表达：

- 它帮助用户记住自己说过什么、做过什么、想过什么。
- 它帮助用户看见自己的问题如何演化。
- 它帮助用户识别长期偏好、长期约束、长期目标。
- 它帮助用户把零散经历沉淀成更稳定的人生结构。

所以它的终局叙事不只是“记忆”，而是：

> 用长期记忆和持续复盘，支持一个人不断靠近自己的理想自我。

---

## 4. 对 OpenMnemo 当前主线最值得吸收的部分

如果把三份文档放回当前仓库主线来看，最值得吸收的不是所有想法一起上，而是分层吸收。

## 4.1 可以立即吸收进主线的

- 把 OpenMnemo 的定位从“归档工具”明确升级为“个人 AI 记忆底座”。
- 把统一 AI Chat 入口视为记忆调用层，而不是单纯问答 UI。
- 把记忆对象继续从 session 往 unit 级演进。
- 把 graph 从展示层进一步升级为检索与关联层。
- 把 heartbeat 视为持续提炼、复盘、进化的后台机制。
- 把“用户原始问题”视为核心资产，而不是只保留 AI 回复。

## 4.2 应作为中期设计牵引，但不应现在插队实现的

- 无限画布式复杂交互工作台
- 更强的主动提醒和长期陪伴式行为
- 多身份、多分区、更细粒度的共享授权模型
- 更完整的数字分身 / 理想自我系统

这些方向是对的，但更适合作为 Phase 1 以后逐步长出来的上层能力。

## 4.3 暂时只保留为远期愿景的

- 复杂的商业化授权体系
- 记忆包分发、版权和防盗版机制
- 链上存证、继承分区、强资产化设计

这些内容可以作为长远想象，但不应干扰当前底座和数据层的收口。

---

## 5. 一句话的分层总结

如果用最简洁的话概括这三份文档：

- 主文档回答的是“OpenMnemo 最终想成为什么”。
- OpenClaw 文档回答的是“记忆应该如何被调出来”。
- Chat Nio 文档回答的是“用户最终会如何用它”。

三者合起来，形成的是一条完整路径：

> 先做一个开放的个人 AI 记忆底座，再用原子化记忆对象和探针式检索把记忆调得准、调得稳，最后通过统一聊天入口、画布、插件、IM 和 Agent 等交互层，把这套底座变成真正可用的人生记忆操作系统。

---

## 6. 最终定位表达

当前最适合作为 OpenMnemo 对外或对内统一表述的一句话是：

> OpenMnemo 不是一个对话归档工具，而是一个开源的个人 AI 记忆底座。它把分散在各平台的对话、想法、决策和经验，沉淀成可调用、可隔离、可共享、可进化的长期记忆中枢，并在此基础上继续生长出统一入口、主动复盘、数字分身与理想自我支持系统。
